chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH KARACHI CRL. BAIL APPLICATION NO. 2333 OF 2023 Applicants: 1) Begum Mai & 2) Muhammad Hayat through Mr. Subhan Javed, Advocate Respondent: The State through Mr. Muhammad Iqbal Awan, Additional Prosecutor General Sindh Date of hearing: 1st November 2023.-.-.-.-. O R D E R Omar Sial, J. Begum Mai and Mohammad Hayat have sought post-arrest bail in crime number 1136 of 2023 registered under sections 371-A and 371-B P.P.C. at the Shah Latif Town police station. Earlier on 25.09.2023, the learned 3rd Additional Sessions Judge, Malir, dismissed the bail application filed by the applicants in that court. 2. The F.I.R. given above was lodged on the complaint of S.I. Yar Mohammad Dars on 17.09.2023. Yar Mohammad, who is a police officer, reported that while patrolling, he received a message that a person named Ali had called the police station and said that a woman was engaged in immoral activities and that some people were beating her. The police arrived at the identified spot, and four persons told Yar Mohammad that in a particular house, immoral activities were taking place. The police arrested two men and two women. The applicants were two of those persons. 3. Despite repeated notices, neither Yar Mohammad nor the four persons who had alleged immoral activities came to court. No evidence of the alleged “immoral activities” is on record. No customer of the alleged immoral services was investigated. Malafide on the part of the complainant, Yar Mohammad, and the four persons who told Yar Mohammad about the immoral activities cannot be ruled out. 4. The case against the applicants is one of further inquiry.

Malafide on the part of the complainant, Yar Mohammad, and the four persons who told Yar Mohammad about the immoral activities cannot be ruled out. 4. The case against the applicants is one of further inquiry. They are admitted to bail subject to their furnishing solvent sureties of Rs. 25,000 each and P.R. Bonds in the like amount subject to the satisfaction of the learned trial court. JUDGE
